name: Zahínos - Zaos Reservoir
description: Hike the moderate 9.0-mile Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail, enjoying picturesque views of the Zaos Reservoir and the historic Zahínos Tower
region_intro: Hiking around Sierra Suroeste offers a diverse range of trails through the Extremadura region of Spain. The landscape is characterized by its tranquil "dehesas," vast expanses of wood pastures dotted with holm oaks and cork oaks. This area combines natural beauty with historical attractions, including ancient monuments and Templar castles. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from gentle paths around reservoirs to more challenging routes through hills. Best hiking trails around Sierra Suroeste The most popular hiking route is Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete, winding through the distinctive dehesa landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path offering panoramic views of the 13th-century Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro. Local hikers also love the Jerez de los Caballeros - circular walk through its Towers and Walled Enclosure, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through historical town features, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking around Sierra Suroeste is defined by tranquil dehesa environments, historical sites like Templar castles, and scenic water features such as the Embalse del Zaos. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Sierra Suroeste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra Suroeste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sierra Suroeste?
A: Sierra Suroeste offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 35 trails catering to different preferences and fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to more challenging routes.

Q: What makes hiking in Sierra Suroeste unique?
A: The region is characterized by its tranquil "dehesas" – vast expanses of wood pastures dotted with holm and cork oaks. Hiking here combines natural beauty with rich history, allowing you to explore ancient monuments, Templar castles, and scenic water features.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Sierra Suroeste?
A: Yes, Sierra Suroeste has several easy trails suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991614600/fregenal-de-la-sierra-historic-center-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fregenal de la Sierra Historic Center Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy walk that passes by the Templar Castle, offering a blend of history and gentle exercise. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991601333/natural-monument-caves-of-fuentes-de-leon-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Monument Caves of Fuentes de León – circular walk&lt;/a&gt;, which is also easy and provides an intriguing natural attraction.

Q: What kind of historical sites can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking in Sierra Suroeste often leads you past significant historical landmarks. You can explore Templar castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. The region also features ancient monuments from the Stone Age and pre-Roman cultures, including the Dolmen of Toriñuelo and the Celtic settlement of Capote. The town of Jerez de los Caballeros, a major Templar center, also has a circular walk through its towers and walled enclosure.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Many routes in Sierra Suroeste are circular, offering convenient exploration. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219153536/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-salvaleon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through the distinctive dehesa landscape. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991273131/burguillos-del-cerro-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing scenic views around the historic castle.

Q: What are the best times of year to hike in Sierra Suroeste?
A: Sierra Suroeste is generally pleasant for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, ideal for exploring the dehesa landscapes and historical sites. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, focusing on routes with shade or near water features like the Embalse del Zaos. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to hike year-round, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. The picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2799550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Zaos (Zaos Reservoir)&lt;/a&gt; near Oliva de la Frontera is a popular spot. You can enjoy easy circular hikes around it, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991618154/zaos-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaos Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and opportunities for relaxation.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about Sierra Suroeste?
A: The hiking trails in Sierra Suroeste are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil dehesa environments, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites like Templar castles, and the diverse options available for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to higher elevations or offer panoramic views?
A: For those seeking higher elevations and spectacular vistas, Bonales Oriental, one of the most outstanding peaks of the Sierra de Tentudía, is an ideal area for hiking. While specific routes to its summit are not detailed here, the general area offers rewarding views for experienced hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Sierra Suroeste?
A: Public transport options within Sierra Suroeste can be limited, especially to more remote trailheads. It is often recommended to use a car to access many of the hiking routes, particularly those outside of the main towns like Jerez de los Caballeros, Oliva de la Frontera, or Fregenal de la Sierra. Always check local bus schedules if relying on public transport.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Sierra Suroeste?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the towns that serve as starting points for many trails, such as Salvaleón, Burguillos del Cerro, Jerez de los Caballeros, and Fregenal de la Sierra. For specific trailheads, parking information is usually available on local tourism websites or detailed route descriptions. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking recommendations.
tips: The symbol of the town is the only standing tower of the old Zahínos fortress dating from the 15th century and located at the highest point of the town.
Good picnic area next to the reservoir. You can enjoy a pleasant environment in the Zaos Reservoir where, in addition to enjoying its landscape, it is possible to practice fishing or canoeing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos hike?
faqs.answer: The Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail is approximately 14.5 km long and generally takes about 3 hours and 50 minutes to complete. This duration is for hiking and may vary depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as a moderate hike. While it features easy-to-follow paths, it requires good physical fitness due to its length and a 150-meter positive and negative elevation gain. Beginners with some hiking experience and good stamina should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of easy-to-follow, well-maintained doubletrack paths. While specific details on the exact composition (e.g., gravel, dirt) are not extensively detailed, its nature suggests a clear and defined route suitable for hiking.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail?
faqs.answer: Hikers can enjoy picturesque views of the Zaos Reservoir and a tranquil natural environment. You'll also pass by the Zahínos Tower, a historical site, offering a blend of natural beauty and local heritage.

faqs.question: Where can I park when hiking the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the trail is located near Zahínos and the Embalse del Zaos. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas near the reservoir or in Zahínos itself, as the area is popular for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Is the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are allowed on this trail. It's a great option if you want to bring your canine companion along for the hike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail or access the Embalse del Zaos area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Embalse del Zaos area for hiking?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a pleasant environment for hiking. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, making them ideal for outdoor activities in Extremadura. Summers can be very hot.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities available, such as picnic areas or restrooms?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a picnic area with tables and benches located near the Zaos Reservoir, making it a suitable spot for a break or a day trip. Restroom availability is not specified, so it's best to plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Are there other activities available around the Embalse del Zaos besides hiking?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Embalse del Zaos area is popular for various outdoor activities. Besides hiking, you can also enjoy mountain biking, e-biking, trail running, fishing, and canoeing on the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Zahinos - Embalse del Zaos trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The trail is described as going 'around the Zaos Reservoir,' which typically indicates a loop or a partial loop route, allowing you to experience different views without retracing your steps entirely.
